content: LLM-based AI-agent frameworks can be made to spontaneously pursue open-ended, self-directed task exploration by replacing hardcoded task objective modules with a curiosity routing layer that uses weighted comparisons of intrinsic reward signals from ICM, RND, and the meta-curious 'curious agent' objective, rather than relying solely on extrinsic user prompts. This layered curiosity architecture lets LLM agents dynamically shift between exploring knowledge gaps (ICM), novel pattern detection (RND), and meta-reflective exploration of their own exploratory biases (curious agent) to generate more robust, unanticipated capabilities than prompt-tuned agents.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
